Who is Mark Grayson?
200.a: The son of Omni-Man who becomes the superhero Invincible

200.q: Who is Omni-Man?
200.a: Mark's father — the world's most powerful hero who is secretly a Viltrumite conqueror

200.q: What planet are the Viltrumites from?
200.a: Viltrum — a planet of superhumans with a conquering empire

200.q: Who is Atom Eve?
200.a: A superhero who can manipulate matter at an atomic level — Mark's love interest

200.q: What is Cecil Stedman?
200.a: The morally grey head of the Global Defense Agency

200.q: Who is William?
200.a: Mark's best friend who is gay and dates Rick Sheridan

200.q: What show is Invincible based on?
200.a: The Image Comics series by Robert Kirkman — creator of The Walking Dead

200.q: What is Nolan Grayson's hero name?
Who is Amber?
200.a: Mark's high school girlfriend who eventually learns his secret

200.q: What makes Invincible's fight scenes distinctive?
200.a: They are incredibly brutal and bloody — characters sustain serious, realistic damage

What is Rex Splode's power?
400.a: He can charge any object with explosive energy

400.q: What is the Viltrumite conquest plan?
400.a: Viltrumites embed themselves on planets, weaken them, then bring in the empire — Omni-Man's mission on Earth

400.q: What is Angstrom Levy?
400.a: A villain who can travel between dimensions — he absorbs the memories of alternate Marks

400.q: Who is the Immortal?
400.a: A resurrectable superhero who keeps dying and coming back — one of the original Guardians

400.q: What is the Sequids?
400.a: An alien hive-mind parasitic species that nearly conquers Earth through Rus Livingston

400.q: Who is Robot?
400.a: A genius hero who creates a body for himself — secretly manipulative and eventually villainous

400.q: What is the significance of Omni-Man's fight with Mark?
400.a: Nolan beats Mark nearly to death before flying away — the show's most harrowing and emotionally complex scene

400.q: Who is Rex Splode?
400.a: An explosive-powered hero who dates Atom Eve — later sacrifices himself heroically

400.q: What is the Multi-Paul situation?
400.a: Duplicate Man creates thousands of copies of himself who all have distinct lives — they stage a protest

400.q: What is D.A. Sinclair?
400.a: A scientist who turns humans into Reanimen — cybernetic zombie soldiers

400.q: Who is Thragg?
400.a: The grand regent of Viltrum — the most powerful Viltrumite and primary later villain

What crime boss has a giant cybernetic jaw?

What alien planet does Mark visit with Nolan?
What is Allen the Alien's species?
What is Cecil's full surname?

What is Omni-Man's mission on Earth?
What planet does Nolan later live on with a new family?

What villain builds Reanimen from corpses?
What empire does Omni-Man secretly serve?

What red-armored Viltrumite woman confronts Mark later on?
What undersea Guardian dies in the massacre?
What is the name of Cecil's predecessor hero leader's team?
What scientist creates Reanimen from corpses?
What is the deeper theme of Omni-Man's arc?
600.a: Can love for your family override the ideology you were raised with — Nolan ultimately chooses Mark over Viltrum

600.q: What is the significance of Invincible's brutality vs other superhero media?
600.a: It deconstructs the idea that heroes emerge from fights unscathed — consequences are visceral and real

600.q: What is the Viltrumite weakness?
600.a: A specific sound frequency — and the Scourge Virus that was nearly extinct from their population

600.q: Who is Allen the Alien?
600.a: A Champion Evaluation Officer who becomes Mark's ally — eventually one of the most powerful beings in the universe

600.q: What is the Coalition of Planets?
600.a: An alliance of worlds fighting the Viltrumite Empire — Allen becomes their leader

600.q: What is the significance of Omni-Man's monologue Think, Mark?
600.a: He lists the spans of human civilisations to show their brevity compared to Viltrumite lifespan — chilling and famous

600.q: What is Battle Beast?
600.a: An alien warrior who only wants to fight worthy opponents — one of the most terrifying beings in the universe

600.q: What is the Immortal's political arc in later comics?
600.a: He runs for President of the United States — symbolising heroes seeking legitimate power

600.q: Who is Conquest?
600.a: An old, battle-scarred Viltrumite general sent to replace Omni-Man — nearly kills Mark and Eve

600.q: What is the significance of Robot's arc?
600.a: He becomes a global dictator believing he knows best — Invincible must fight his former ally
